models.name: individuals_any_high_value_order
models.sql: SELECT DISTINCT Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c FROM Individuals__dlm JOIN SalesOrders__dlm ON Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.IndividualId__c WHERE (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c OR (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL)) AND trim(cast(Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c as varchar(255))) <> '' AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c IS NOT NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c > 5000

d360_segment_create.description: Top-level keys form a valid request body for the ANY-MATCH pattern. additionalExamples shows other canonical SQL shapes — copy a model.sql into the body above to reuse them. Always project PK + KQ of segmentOnApiName; join ON-clauses must be a single EQUALS between two qualified identifiers (no extra ANDed predicates). INNER joins should reproduce the engine's defensive PK+KQ join semantics in WHERE: (a) NULL-safe KQ equality '(t1.KQ_x = t2.KQ_x OR (t1.KQ_x IS NULL AND t2.KQ_x IS NULL))', (b) empty-string PK guard 'trim(cast(t1.PK as varchar(N))) <> ''', (c) IS NOT NULL on filter fields before value comparisons. EXCEPTION — anti-match (LEFT JOIN + HAVING SUM(CASE)=0): do NOT add KQ-parity, trim, or IS NOT NULL clauses to WHERE. Any predicate referencing the right side in WHERE collapses LEFT JOIN to INNER and breaks 'no related row' semantics; the trim/IS NOT NULL guards belong in the CASE expression, not WHERE.

additionalExamples.name: simple-attribute-equality
additionalExamples.intent: Filter the segment-on entity directly by a column value (no joins). Add the empty-string PK guard for parity with engine output.

model.name: individuals_first_name_equals
model.sql: SELECT Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c FROM Individuals__dlm WHERE Individuals__dlm.FirstName__c = 'Robot' AND trim(cast(Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c as varchar(255))) <> ''

additionalExamples.name: any-match-related
additionalExamples.intent: Include individuals who have at least one related row matching a predicate (existence check). DISTINCT is needed because INNER JOIN can multiply rows. INNER JOIN — include all three engine-parity guards in WHERE: (a) NULL-safe KQ equality, (b) empty-string PK guard, (c) IS NOT NULL on the filter field before the value comparison.

model.name: individuals_any_high_value_order
model.sql: SELECT DISTINCT Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c FROM Individuals__dlm JOIN SalesOrders__dlm ON Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.IndividualId__c WHERE (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c OR (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL)) AND trim(cast(Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c as varchar(255))) <> '' AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c IS NOT NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c > 5000

additionalExamples.name: aggregate-sum-threshold
additionalExamples.intent: Include individuals whose aggregate over related rows passes a threshold (e.g. lifetime spend > 1000). GROUP BY the projected PK + KQ; put the aggregate test in HAVING. INNER JOIN — include the engine-parity WHERE guards (KQ-parity, empty-PK trim, IS NOT NULL on the aggregated field).

model.name: individuals_total_spend_over_1000
model.sql: SELECT Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c FROM Individuals__dlm JOIN SalesOrders__dlm ON Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.IndividualId__c WHERE (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c OR (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL)) AND trim(cast(Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c as varchar(255))) <> '' AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c IS NOT NULL GROUP BY Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c HAVING SUM(SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c) > 1000

additionalExamples.name: aggregate-count-threshold
additionalExamples.intent: Include individuals with COUNT of qualifying related rows above a threshold (e.g. more than one large order). Filter qualifying rows in WHERE alongside the engine-parity guards; put the count test in HAVING.

model.name: individuals_more_than_one_large_order
model.sql: SELECT Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c FROM Individuals__dlm JOIN SalesOrders__dlm ON Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.IndividualId__c WHERE (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c OR (Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c IS NULL)) AND trim(cast(Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c as varchar(255))) <> '' AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c IS NOT NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c > 10000 GROUP BY Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c HAVING COUNT(SalesOrders__dlm.SalesOrderId__c) > 1

additionalExamples.name: anti-match-no-related-row
additionalExamples.intent: Include individuals who have NO related row matching a predicate (e.g. no order over 10000). The natural 'LEFT JOIN ... ON pk=fk AND P WHERE id IS NULL' shape is rejected because the ON-clause must be a single EQUALS. Use HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN P THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 instead — LEFT JOIN preserves individuals with zero related rows (CASE = 0 over the synthesized NULL row), and any matching row forces SUM > 0 so the individual is dropped. ENGINE-PARITY DIVERGENCE: do NOT add KQ-parity, empty-PK trim, or IS NOT NULL clauses to WHERE here. The engine puts these guards in the ON-clause of its emitted LEFT JOIN, but rule (4) forbids compound ON. Putting them in WHERE would collapse the LEFT JOIN to INNER (predicates referencing the right side eliminate rows with NULL right) and drop individuals with zero matches, defeating the anti-match. The KQ-parity check is also unneeded here: any individual whose KQ differs from the FK side simply doesn't match the predicate, contributing 0 to SUM, and is correctly kept.

model.name: individuals_no_order_over_10000
model.sql: SELECT Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c FROM Individuals__dlm LEFT JOIN SalesOrders__dlm ON Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c = SalesOrders__dlm.IndividualId__c GROUP BY Individuals__dlm.IndividualId__c, Individuals__dlm.KQ_IndividualId__c HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c IS NOT NULL AND SalesOrders__dlm.TotalAmount__c > 10000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0

d360_ci_create.expression:
SELECT
  Account__dlm.Region__c as region,
  SUM(Opportunity__dlm.Amount__c) as total_revenue,
  COUNT(DISTINCT Opportunity__dlm.ssot__Id__c) as opp_count,
  AVG(Opportunity__dlm.Amount__c) as avg_opportunity_size
FROM Account__dlm
INNER JOIN Opportunity__dlm
  ON Account__dlm.ssot__Id__c = Opportunity__dlm.AccountId__c
WHERE Opportunity__dlm.CloseDate__c >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 365 DAY
GROUP BY Account__dlm.Region__c
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

d360_sdm_create.label: Account Revenue Model
d360_sdm_create.description: Semantic model for account and revenue analysis with calculated metrics

d360_sdm_calc_dim_create.label: Revenue Tier
d360_sdm_calc_dim_create.expression: IF [Account].[AnnualRevenue] > 1000000 THEN 'Enterprise' ELSEIF [Account].[AnnualRevenue] > 100000 THEN 'Mid-Market' ELSE 'SMB' END

d360_ir_create.description: Match individuals by normalized email, then fuzzy name + email
d360_ir_create._note: configurationType REQUIRED: 'individual' or 'account'. matchMethodType values: exact, exactnormalized, fuzzy, fuzzyhigh, fuzzylow. reconciliationRules ruleType values: lastupdated (requires LastModifiedDate mapped), mostfrequent, sourcesequence. IMPORTANT: every reconciliationRule MUST include ruleType — omitting it causes a server 500. For lastupdated, the DMO must have ssot__LastModifiedDate__c mapped; use sourcesequence if not.
d360_ir_create.label: Email and Name Matching

d360_transform_prepare._note: Simple DCSQL batch transform for validation (single node). For complex multi-node examples, see d360_transform_prepare_dcsql_batch. For streaming, see d360_transform_prepare_sql_streaming.
d360_transform_prepare.name: account_summary
d360_transform_prepare.label: Account Summary
d360_transform_prepare.description: Simple single-node batch transform that filters and enriches account data

d360_transform_prepare_dcsql_batch._note: Advanced DCSQL with window functions and rank-based filtering for top performers analysis
d360_transform_prepare_dcsql_batch.name: top_sales_performers
d360_transform_prepare_dcsql_batch.label: Top Sales Performers by Region
d360_transform_prepare_dcsql_batch.description: Identifies top-performing sales reps with rankings and percentile calculations

sales_with_metrics.name: sales_with_metrics
sales_with_metrics.compiled_code: SELECT "OwnerId__c", "Owner_Region__c", COUNT(*) as "DealCount__c", SUM("Amount__c") as "TotalRevenue__c", AVG("Amount__c") as "AvgDealSize__c", MAX("CloseDate__c") as "LastWinDate__c" FROM "Opportunity__dlm" WHERE "IsWon__c" = true AND "CloseDate__c" >= DATEADD(quarter, -1, CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY "OwnerId__c", "Owner_Region__c"

ranked_performers.name: ranked_performers
ranked_performers.compiled_code: SELECT "OwnerId__c", "Owner_Region__c", "DealCount__c", "TotalRevenue__c", "AvgDealSize__c", "LastWinDate__c", ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "Owner_Region__c" ORDER BY "TotalRevenue__c" DESC) as "RegionRank__c", PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY "Owner_Region__c" ORDER BY "TotalRevenue__c") as "RegionPercentile__c", ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY "TotalRevenue__c" DESC) as "GlobalRank__c", CASE WHEN PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY "Owner_Region__c" ORDER BY "TotalRevenue__c") >= 0.9 THEN 'Top 10%' WHEN PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY "Owner_Region__c" ORDER BY "TotalRevenue__c") >= 0.75 THEN 'Top 25%' WHEN PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY "Owner_Region__c" ORDER BY "TotalRevenue__c") >= 0.50 THEN 'Top 50%' ELSE 'Below Average' END as "PerformanceTier__c" FROM sales_with_metrics WHERE "DealCount__c" >= 3

d360_transform_prepare_sql_streaming._note: Real-time lead enrichment with INNER JOIN. Streaming SQL supports: INNER JOIN, CASE/COALESCE, string/date functions, row-by-row transformations. Does NOT support: LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER JOIN, aggregations (COUNT, SUM, AVG), window functions (ROW_NUMBER, RANK), GROUP BY, subqueries.
d360_transform_prepare_sql_streaming.name: realtime_lead_account_enrichment
d360_transform_prepare_sql_streaming.label: Real-Time Lead with Account Enrichment
d360_transform_prepare_sql_streaming.description: Enriches incoming leads with account data via INNER JOIN plus calculated scores and routing

definition.expression: SELECT l."Id__c", l."Email__c", l."FirstName__c", l."LastName__c", l."Company__c", l."Title__c", l."LeadSource__c", l."Phone__c", l."CreatedDate__c", a."Id__c" as "AccountId__c", a."Name__c" as "AccountName__c", a."Industry__c" as "AccountIndustry__c", a."AnnualRevenue__c" as "AccountRevenue__c", a."NumberOfEmployees__c" as "AccountEmployees__c", a."Type__c" as "AccountType__c", a."Rating__c" as "AccountRating__c", CASE WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%VP%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Vice President%' THEN 'VP' WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Director%' THEN 'Director' WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Manager%' THEN 'Manager' WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%CEO%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Chief%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%President%' THEN 'C-Level' ELSE 'Individual Contributor' END as "Seniority__c", CASE WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%VP%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Chief%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%President%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Director%' THEN 30 WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Manager%' THEN 20 ELSE 10 END + CASE WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 50000000 THEN 25 WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 10000000 THEN 20 WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 1000000 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END + CASE WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 1000 THEN 20 WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 500 THEN 15 WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 100 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END + CASE WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Referral' THEN 20 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Partner' THEN 15 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Event' THEN 10 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Web' THEN 5 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN a."Rating__c" = 'Hot' THEN 15 WHEN a."Rating__c" = 'Warm' THEN 10 ELSE 5 END as "CompositeScore__c", CASE WHEN (CASE WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%VP%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Chief%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%President%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Director%' THEN 30 WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Manager%' THEN 20 ELSE 10 END + CASE WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 50000000 THEN 25 WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 10000000 THEN 20 WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 1000000 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END + CASE WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 1000 THEN 20 WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 500 THEN 15 WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 100 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END + CASE WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Referral' THEN 20 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Partner' THEN 15 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Event' THEN 10 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Web' THEN 5 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN a."Rating__c" = 'Hot' THEN 15 WHEN a."Rating__c" = 'Warm' THEN 10 ELSE 5 END) >= 80 THEN 'Hot' WHEN (CASE WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%VP%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Chief%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%President%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Director%' THEN 30 WHEN l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Manager%' THEN 20 ELSE 10 END + CASE WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 50000000 THEN 25 WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 10000000 THEN 20 WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 1000000 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END + CASE WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 1000 THEN 20 WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 500 THEN 15 WHEN a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 100 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END + CASE WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Referral' THEN 20 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Partner' THEN 15 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Event' THEN 10 WHEN l."LeadSource__c" = 'Web' THEN 5 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN a."Rating__c" = 'Hot' THEN 15 WHEN a."Rating__c" = 'Warm' THEN 10 ELSE 5 END) >= 60 THEN 'Warm' ELSE 'Cold' END as "Priority__c", CASE WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 100000000 AND (l."Title__c" ILIKE '%VP%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%Chief%' OR l."Title__c" ILIKE '%President%') THEN 'Strategic_Accounts' WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 50000000 OR a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 1000 THEN 'Enterprise_Sales' WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 10000000 OR a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 500 THEN 'Commercial_Sales' WHEN a."AnnualRevenue__c" > 1000000 OR a."NumberOfEmployees__c" > 50 THEN 'Mid_Market_Sales' ELSE 'SMB_Sales' END as "OwnerQueue__c", CASE WHEN a."Type__c" = 'Customer' THEN 'Existing_Customer_Expansion' WHEN a."Type__c" = 'Partner' THEN 'Partner_Led_Opportunity' WHEN a."Type__c" = 'Competitor' THEN 'Competitive_Displacement' ELSE 'New_Business' END as "OpportunityType__c", CASE WHEN l."Email__c" IS NOT NULL AND l."Phone__c" IS NOT NULL AND a."Industry__c" IS NOT NULL THEN 'Complete' WHEN l."Email__c" IS NULL OR l."Phone__c" IS NULL THEN 'Missing_Contact_Info' WHEN a."Industry__c" IS NULL THEN 'Missing_Firmographic' ELSE 'Partial' END as "DataQuality__c", COALESCE(a."Name__c", l."Company__c", 'Unknown') as "CompanyNormalized__c", UPPER(CONCAT(LEFT(l."FirstName__c", 1), LEFT(l."LastName__c", 1))) as "Initials__c", CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as "EnrichedTimestamp__c" FROM "Lead__dll" l INNER JOIN "Account__dlm" a ON LOWER(TRIM(l."Company__c")) = LOWER(TRIM(a."Name__c"))

d360_transform_create_dcsql_batch._note: Complete create payload with enriched outputDataObjects (after running d360_transform_prepare). Shows isPrimaryKey designation and full field metadata.
d360_transform_create_dcsql_batch.name: customer_360_enrichment
d360_transform_create_dcsql_batch.label: Customer 360 Enrichment
d360_transform_create_dcsql_batch.description: Aggregates customer data from accounts, opportunities, cases, and activities into a unified view

opportunity_metrics.name: opportunity_metrics
opportunity_metrics.compiled_code: SELECT "AccountId__c", COUNT(*) as "OpportunityCount__c", SUM("Amount__c") as "TotalPipeline__c", AVG("Amount__c") as "AvgDealSize__c", MAX("CloseDate__c") as "LastOpportunityDate__c" FROM "Opportunity__dlm" WHERE "IsClosed__c" = false GROUP BY "AccountId__c"

case_metrics.name: case_metrics
case_metrics.compiled_code: SELECT "AccountId__c", COUNT(*) as "OpenCaseCount__c", COUNT(CASE WHEN "Priority__c" = 'High' THEN 1 END) as "HighPriorityCases__c", AVG(DATEDIFF(day, "CreatedDate__c", COALESCE("ClosedDate__c", CURRENT_DATE))) as "AvgCaseResolutionDays__c" FROM "Case__dlm" GROUP BY "AccountId__c"

activity_summary.name: activity_summary
activity_summary.compiled_code: SELECT "AccountId__c", COUNT(*) as "ActivityCount__c", MAX("ActivityDate__c") as "LastActivityDate__c", COUNT(CASE WHEN "ActivityType__c" = 'Email' THEN 1 END) as "EmailCount__c", COUNT(CASE WHEN "ActivityType__c" = 'Meeting' THEN 1 END) as "MeetingCount__c" FROM "Activity__dlm" WHERE "ActivityDate__c" >= DATEADD(month, -6, CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY "AccountId__c"

customer_360_enriched.name: customer_360_enriched
customer_360_enriched.compiled_code: SELECT a."Id__c", a."Name__c", a."Industry__c", a."AnnualRevenue__c", COALESCE(o."OpportunityCount__c", 0) as "OpportunityCount__c", COALESCE(o."TotalPipeline__c", 0) as "TotalPipeline__c", COALESCE(o."AvgDealSize__c", 0) as "AvgDealSize__c", o."LastOpportunityDate__c", COALESCE(c."OpenCaseCount__c", 0) as "OpenCaseCount__c", COALESCE(c."HighPriorityCases__c", 0) as "HighPriorityCases__c", c."AvgCaseResolutionDays__c", COALESCE(act."ActivityCount__c", 0) as "RecentActivityCount__c", act."LastActivityDate__c", COALESCE(act."EmailCount__c", 0) as "EmailCount__c", COALESCE(act."MeetingCount__c", 0) as "MeetingCount__c", CASE WHEN COALESCE(act."ActivityCount__c", 0) >= 10 AND COALESCE(o."TotalPipeline__c", 0) > 100000 THEN 'High' WHEN COALESCE(act."ActivityCount__c", 0) >= 5 OR COALESCE(o."TotalPipeline__c", 0) > 50000 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END as "EngagementScore__c", CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as "EnrichmentTimestamp__c" FROM account_base a LEFT JOIN opportunity_metrics o ON a."Id__c" = o."AccountId__c" LEFT JOIN case_metrics c ON a."Id__c" = c."AccountId__c" LEFT JOIN activity_summary act ON a."Id__c" = act."AccountId__c"

d360_transform_create_sql_streaming.name: streaming_transform
d360_transform_create_sql_streaming.label: streaming transform

definition.expression: SELECT Account_Home__dll.Id__c as Id__c FROM Account_Home__dll

d360_transform_create_sql_streaming._note: Streaming SQL transform with outputDataObjects. Use for real-time row-by-row transformations.

d360_transform_create._note: Simple DCSQL batch transform (single node). For complex multi-node examples, see d360_transform_create_dcsql_batch. For streaming, see d360_transform_create_sql_streaming.
d360_transform_create.name: account_summary
d360_transform_create.label: Account Summary
d360_transform_create.description: Simple single-node batch transform that filters and enriches account data

d360_activation_create.name: Send High Value Customers to Salesforce

d360_dmo_create_engagement.name: WebActivity__dlm
d360_dmo_create_engagement.label: Web Activity Events
d360_dmo_create_engagement._note: For Engagement DMOs, set eventDateColumn to an immutable DateTime field like CreatedDate__c when creating the data stream

d360_dmo_mapping_create._note: IMPORTANT: dataspace query param is REQUIRED (pass via 'dataspace' tool parameter). DLO field names include __c suffix (e.g., Id__c not Id). Use d360_dlo_get to get exact field names. For custom DMOs, you must create the DMO with explicit dataSpaceName first — otherwise the mapping API returns 'DMO not found'. System fields (DataSource__c, DataSourceObject__c, KQ_*) are auto-added.

d360_dmo_mapping_create_contact_point_em._note: For ContactPointEmail, map the source person ID to both ssot__Id__c and ssot__PartyId__c. The PartyId links the email to the Individual for identity resolution. KQ_PartyId__c is auto-added.

d360_datastream_create_sfdc_profile.label: Account Data Stream
d360_datastream_create_sfdc_profile._note: Profile category streams do NOT need an eventDateColumn. The refreshMode UPSERT means new records are inserted and existing records are updated based on the primary key.

d360_datastream_create_sfdc_engagement.label: Task Engagement Stream
d360_datastream_create_sfdc_engagement._note: CRITICAL: Engagement streams MUST set eventDateColumn to an immutable date field (like CreatedDate). Do NOT use LastModifiedDate — it changes on updates and breaks time-series partitioning. Use d360_smart_datastream to auto-select the best field.

d360_datastream_create_csv.label: Customer CSV Upload
d360_datastream_create_csv._note: CSV uploads use UploadedFiles connector. refreshMode TOTAL_REPLACE replaces all data on each refresh. Field names come from CSV headers.

d360_datastream_create_other.label: Product Catalog Stream
d360_datastream_create_other._note: Other category is for reference/lookup data that doesn't fit Profile or Engagement. No eventDateColumn needed.

d360_datastream_create_aws_s3.name: AirlineBookings_S3
d360_datastream_create_aws_s3.label: Airline Bookings (S3)
d360_datastream_create_aws_s3._note: AWS S3 data streams use the DataConnector connectorType with datasource AwsS3 and datastreamType CONNECTORSFRAMEWORK. The connectorDetails name should match your S3 connection name. sourceFields and mappings are required for S3 connectors.

d360_smart_mapping_suggest._note: sourceFields and targetFields are arrays of FieldInput objects. fieldOverrides are FieldOverrideInput objects with source/target field names.

d360_preview_field_matches._note: sourceFields and targetFields are arrays of FieldInput objects with name, optional label, and optional dataType.

d360_smart_datastream._note: Pass an Engagement data stream body and the tool will analyze date fields, rank them, and inject the best eventDateColumn (e.g., CreatedDate over LastModifiedDate).

d360_dataaction_create.name: Send Welcome Email
d360_dataaction_create.description: Send personalized welcome email to new customers

d360_datastream_create_third_party_conne._note: Third-party connectors (Airtable, HubSpot, Marketo, Google Ads, etc.) use datastreamType CONNECTORSFRAMEWORK and connectorType DataConnector. The tool auto-generates data stream name as '{objectName}_{connectionName}', DLO name with __dll suffix, sourceFields, mappings, and dataLakeObjectInfo from the field definitions. Field dataType values from d360_connection_object_fields_describe (VARCHAR→Text, FLOAT→Number, TIMESTAMP→DateTime) are normalized automatically. Use the 'format' property on fields when d360_connection_object_fields_describe returns a format (e.g. DateTime fields).

d360_connection_db_schemas_list._note: advancedAttributes is a JSON string; for Snowflake pass the database name. Returns schemas under that database. Use d360_snowflake_connection_list (or d360_connection_list) to find the connectionId.

d360_connection_objects_list._note: Lists tables/objects in a connection. For DB connectors (e.g., Snowflake) scope by database+schema in advancedAttributes — omitting them can return very large result sets. Omit advancedAttributes for non-DB connectors (e.g., Salesforce).

d360_connection_object_fields_describe._note: Describes column-level schema (names, types, primary keys) of a single object. Discover resourceName via d360_connection_objects_list first.

d360_prediction_job_def_create.label: pred_job_test
d360_prediction_job_def_create._note: Generic create accepts any of the six type discriminators. Prefer the type-specific tools (d360_prediction_job_def_create_regression, _binary_classification, _multiclass_classification, _sentiment_detection, _topic_classification, _clustering) when possible — they pre-shape the payload and run pre-flight validation. References (model, dataObject, modelField, mappedField) accept either { name } or { id }; this example uses names.

d360_prediction_job_def_create_sentiment._note: model defaults to a reference by name to sfdc_ai__DefaultSalesforceSentimentAnalysis when omitted.

d360_prediction_job_def_create_topic_cla._note: topicLabels: 2-5 distinct entries, each ≤ 100 characters. Up to 3 textFields. model defaults to a reference by name to sfdc_ai__DefaultSalesforceTopicClassification when omitted. Only Static labels are supported today.

d360_prediction_job_def_update._note: Partial-update — only fields set in `request` are changed. For activation toggling alone, prefer d360_prediction_job_def_activate / _deactivate.

d360_prediction_job_def_activate._note: Sends PATCH with body { activationStatus: 'Active' }. Required before the definition can be submitted via d360_prediction_job_run.

d360_prediction_job_def_deactivate._note: Sends PATCH with body { activationStatus: 'Inactive' }. Inactive definitions cannot be run.

d360_prediction_job_run._note: Provide either { name: "..." } or { id: "..." }. Definition must be in Active status — call d360_prediction_job_def_activate first if needed. Response carries an initial status (Submited, Pending, NotStarted, or InProgress); poll d360_prediction_job_def_get to see lastRunStatus update.

d360_ml_predict._note: Synchronous inference. model is a configured model ref (id or developer name) — NOT a model artifact. Discover candidate models from d360_ml_configured_model_list.

d360_ml_setup_version_inspector_metrics_._note: Returns inspectorResponses with overview (auc/gini/mcc/mse/rmse + 100-threshold confusionMatrices), outcomeDistribution.training, and gainTable.{training,validation}.

d360_ml_alert_update._note: accepted=true flags the alert for directive auto-application when the next setup-version is created with sourceSetupVersionNumber set to the current version. Use dismissed=true to suppress without applying.

request.label: Attrition Risk Predictor (v1)
request.description: Superseded by Attrition_Risk_Predictor_v2__mla — kept for audit.

d360_ml_model_artifact_update._note: Partial update. PATCH blocked on OOTB. Status (not shown) accepts only Enabled/Disabled — Draft/Deprecated/Rerouted are rejected by the server. featureOverrideValues are upsert-by-sourceValue: existing matched, new inserted, omitted ones removed.

request.label: Attrition Predictor (Catboost)
request.description: Production-grade configured model wrapping the trained Attrition artifact.

d360_ml_configured_model_create._note: artifact is required. Discover the artifact name from the published setup-version's modelArtifact field, or from d360_ml_configured_model_list with assetType=ModelArtifact. Status defaults to Disabled — call d360_ml_configured_model_activate after create. Notable response fields: status, lastActivatedDate, modelDisabledReasons, parameters, inputFields, outputFields.

d360_ml_configured_model_update._note: Partial update. parameterOverrides has full-replace semantics — pass the complete desired list. PATCH on OOTB (Salesforce-provided) models requires visibility.

d360_ml_configured_model_activate._note: Sets status=Enabled. Required before any prediction-job definition referencing this model can run. Response includes lastActivatedBy and lastActivatedDate populated by the platform.

d360_ml_configured_model_history_list._note: Snapshots are content-based — no entry for no-op edits. OOTB models have no history.

d360_ml_query_outcome._note: Omit predictionType to return all supervised candidates.

request.label: Attrition Risk Predictor
request.description: Predict whether an employee will leave the company.

d360_ml_model_setup_create._note: outcomeDefinition is immutable after creation. goal is Minimize or Maximize; fieldNames lists the outcome field(s).

request.description: Updated description for the attrition predictor.

d360_ml_model_setup_update._note: Partial update. Settable fields: label, description, modelCapability, outcomeDefinition.

d360_ml_setup_version_create._note: Pass each field configuration as returned by d360_ml_query_setup_fields.

d360_ml_setup_version_update._note: Container can be referenced by api name (e.g. Attrition_Risk_Predictor) or id. versionId must always be an id. Get the id from d360_ml_setup_version_list or the response of d360_ml_setup_version_create.

d360_p13n_transformer_create.name: promo_banner_transformer
d360_p13n_transformer_create.label: Promotional Banner Transformer
d360_p13n_transformer_create.description: Renders personalized promotional banners with dynamic content

bannerTitle.label: Banner Title
bannerTitle.description: Main heading text for the banner

bannerImage.label: Banner Image URL
bannerImage.description: URL to the banner background image

d360_p13n_transformer_create._note: Substitution definitions are map-based: key = variable name, value = SubstitutionInputDetails. ConfigType values: SchemaPath (data from personalization point), Static (hardcoded value). TransformerType values: Handlebars (HTML template), HtmlElementModifier (DOM manipulation), AgentScript (script-based), Component (mobile component name).

d360_p13n_experience_config_create.name: homepage_hero_banner
d360_p13n_experience_config_create.label: Homepage Hero Banner Personalization
d360_p13n_experience_config_create.description: Shows personalized hero banner on homepage based on user preferences
d360_p13n_experience_config_create._note: Complete experience config structure. DataProvider links to a personalization point (recommendation engine output). SourceMatchers define where to apply (URL patterns, content zones). TransformationConfig defines rendering: 'when' values (Immediately, ExitIntent, ElementClick, ScrollPercent), 'method' values (ReplaceContentZoneContent, ReplaceElementContent, AddAfterElement, AddBeforeElement, AddOverlay, ContentZoneHandler). Transformations array applies one or more transformers with specific substitution values. Use disableRelatedExperiences=true query param to disable other experiences in same content zone.

d360_p13n_mobile_preview_create._note: Generate mobile preview link for testing. Include either 'experienceConfig' or 'transformer' (not both). DecisionRequestBody provides test context: 'p' contains decision/cohort/experiment IDs for A/B testing, 'c' contains individual/anchor IDs for personalization. Response includes previewUrl (time-limited) and expiresAt timestamp.

d360_p13n_experience_config_update.description: Updated description for the experience config
d360_p13n_experience_config_update._note: Partial update — only include fields to change. All fields are optional. Common updates: isEnabled (enable/disable), description, sourceMatchers, transformationConfig. Use disableRelatedExperiences=true query param to disable other experiences in same content zone.

d360_p13n_transformer_update.label: Updated Banner Transformer
d360_p13n_transformer_update._note: Partial update — only include fields to change. All fields are optional. Note: Changing transformerType may require updating transformerTypeDetails to match the new type.

d360_p13n_schema_create.name: product_recommendations_schema
d360_p13n_schema_create.label: Product Recommendations Schema
d360_p13n_schema_create.description: Schema for product recommendation content with custom attributes
d360_p13n_schema_create._note: Creates a personalization schema that defines content structure. personalizationType values: ExperienceVariation (A/B test variations), FlowPath (flow branches), ManualContent (manually curated), Recommendations (ML-driven). contentObject specifies the main DMO and its fields. attributes define custom variables for decisions. relatedContentObjects enable nested data access. Prerequisites: dataSpace and contentObject DMO must exist.

d360_p13n_schema_update.label: Updated Product Recommendations
d360_p13n_schema_update._note: Partial update — only include fields to change. All fields are optional. Can add new attributes, update contentObject fields, or modify relatedContentObjects. Changes affect existing personalization points using this schema.

d360_p13n_point_create.name: home_page_experience
d360_p13n_point_create.label: Home Page Experience Point
d360_p13n_point_create.description: Personalization point for customizing home page experience based on user segment

decisions.name: default_decision
decisions.label: Default Decision
decisions.description: Fallback decision for all other users

d360_p13n_point_create._note: Creates a personalization point that determines which experience/content to show. source values: BlockBuilder (Email Builder blocks), ExperienceBuilder (Digital Experiences), FlowBuilder (Flow decisions), PersonalizationApp (Data Cloud P13N UI). schemaName references a custom schema; use schemaEnum for standard schemas (DecisionDefined, ExperienceVariation, FlowPath). decisions array defines prioritized rules with criteria (JSON filter syntax) and attributeValues for personalization variables. isAuthenticationRequired controls whether API calls require user authentication. Prerequisites: dataSpace, profileDataGraph, and schema must exist.

d360_p13n_point_update.name: home_page_experience
d360_p13n_point_update.label: Updated Home Page Experience
d360_p13n_point_update._note: Full replacement (PUT) — must provide all required fields. Cannot change name after creation. Use to modify decision logic, update authentication requirements, change schema associations, or adjust maxItemsCount. Changes take effect immediately for runtime execution. Decision order matters: first matching criteria wins.

d360_p13n_engagement_signals_list._note: Both parameters are required. dataSpaceId is the isolated workspace (use 'default' unless you have multiple spaces). dataGraphId is the profile or item data graph to query engagement signals for.

d360_p13n_org_info_get._note: No parameters required. Returns the Data Cloud Tenant Specific Endpoint (dcTse) for your organization. Use this first to confirm the correct Data Cloud endpoint before configuring personalization tools.

d360_p13n_experience_config_list._note: Only idOrAppSourceIdOrName is required. Use personalizationPointNameOrId or transformerNameOrId to filter results. Set isWpmUrlRequired=true to get Web Personalization Manager edit URLs in the response.

d360_p13n_experience_config_get._note: Both idOrAppSourceIdOrName (data connector) and nameParam (experience config name) are required. Set isWpmUrlRequired=true to include a Web Personalization Manager edit URL in the response.

d360_p13n_experience_config_delete._note: WARNING: Cannot be undone. Provide the data connector ID/name and the exact experience config name. This removes the personalization configuration and stops delivery to the application.

d360_p13n_transformer_list._note: All parameters are optional. source values: Global, Organization (default). channelType values: MobileApp, WebApp. transformerType values: Handlebars, HtmlElementModifier, AgentScript, Component. transformerCategory values: EmbeddedContent, Agent. Set isDataConnectorAttached=false to find unattached transformers.

d360_p13n_transformer_get._note: Provide the transformer name or ID. Returns complete transformer including substitution definitions, type details, and associated data connectors.

d360_p13n_transformer_delete._note: WARNING: Cannot be undone. Breaking change — any experience configs referencing this transformer will stop working. Verify no active experiences are using this transformer before deleting.

d360_p13n_schema_get._note: Provide the schema name or ID. Returns complete schema including all attributes, content object configuration, and related content objects.

d360_p13n_schema_delete._note: WARNING: Cannot be undone. Any personalization points referencing this schema will break. Verify no active personalization points use this schema before deleting.

d360_p13n_point_get._note: Provide the personalization point name or ID. Returns complete point including all decisions with attribute values and criteria, schema configuration, authentication settings, and current sync status.

d360_p13n_point_delete._note: WARNING: Cannot be undone. Immediately stops personalization at this point. Any references from experiments or batch decisions will break. Verify no active usage before deleting.
